title: noccaea caerulescens subsp. firmiensis
intro: Noccaea caerulescens subsp. firmiensis, also known as Alpine Penny-cress, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mustard family, characterized by its blue-green leaves and small white flowers. Traditionally, Alpine Penny-cress has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its high concentration of glucosinolates. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, supporting immune function, and potentially preventing certain types of cancer. Alpine Penny-cress can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential interactions with medications and other health conditions.
